figure.title: UALink TL Conceptual Diagram
figure.purpose: Provides a high-level conceptual view of the UALink Transaction Layer (TL) and its interfaces with the UPLI Originator and Completer.

technical_summary: The UALink TL sits between the external UPLI blocks and the lower layers (DL/PL).
It processes 64-Byte Inbound Receive (Rx) TL Flits and Outbound Transmit (Tx) TL Flits.
The internal architecture consists of an internal UPLI Originator and UPLI Completer interfacing with the corresponding external blocks.
Red lines denote Request (Req) and Originator Data (OrigData) channels.
Blue lines denote Read Response/Data (Rd Rsp/Data) and Write Response (Wr Rsp) channels.

interfaces.name: UPLI
interfaces.description: Interface between the TL and the upper protocol layers.

packet_layout.description: Standard data unit transferred to and from the TL.

critical_facts: The UALink Transaction Layer operates fundamentally on 64-byte flits for both inbound and outbound traffic.
